text:
Market today: Mixed but leaning constructive for chips — Micron's blowout earnings are the real driver today, lifting the semiconductor space. Bond flows surging signals some defensive rotation, so the rally may stay narrow. Apple dropping 6% on memory price hikes is a cross-current worth watching.

Your holdings: PLTR is your biggest problem — down nearly 18% and on a SELL signal; on margin, that hole gets deeper fast. AVGO is your largest position and down 13% with a TRIM flag — Micron's pop could give you a brief window to reduce at better prices. SPAL is a 2x leveraged ETF already down 18% and barely above its stop at $19 — decay and leverage are eating you alive there. SPCX is a brand-new, very volatile IPO with almost no track record; treat it as fragile.

Watch: (1) Whether Micron's momentum carries AVGO and SMH higher — that's your exit/trim window. (2) Apple's memory-crunch narrative; if it spreads, it pressures chip demand sentiment.

Bottom line: Use the Micron-driven chip bounce today to trim AVGO and cut PLTR — margin means you can't afford to wait for a full recovery.
